items.title: 26-year-old shot and killed by ICE agent in Maine sparks demonstrations
items.description: US ICE agents shot and killed a 26-year-old Colombian man in Maine early Monday morning. Department of Homeland Security secretary Markwayne Mullin first said that the man was a target of an ICE investigation and that he had attempted to "weaponise" his vehicle. Later the story changed – it was confirmed that the man was not the target of the investigation, and DHS described his car as "attempting to flee the scene". Simon Moritz sheds light on the situation that has sparked anti-ICE demonstrations in the area.

items.title: France holds largest military parade ever in large show of unity for Ukraine

items.title: Paramount's Warner takeover challenged by 12 US states
items.description: California is leading the legal charge against one of the world's biggest media mergers ever. Twelve US states have filed a lawsuit seeking to block Paramount Skydance from acquiring Warner Brothers Discovery – saying the deal would create a company with too much market power and end up harming consumers and workers. The legal challenge is being seen in part as a political proxy battle – with Democratic state officials seeking to rein in what many see as unchecked crony capitalism and corruption based on business leaders' proximity to President Donald Trump. Details by Bryan Quinn.

items.title: New York to impose the country’s first state-wide moratorium on data centres
items.description: New York will block new large data centers that fuel artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid

items.title: Australia's youth ban on social media has minimal results
items.description: Since coming into effect in December, an Australian law has barred kids under 16 from having accounts on platforms like Snapchat, Instagram and YouTube. Yet, studies show that most children are still able to access and create accounts on the networks. The government has doubled the maximum fine for non-compliance by tech firms, and is warning of further court action. Story by Bryan Quinn.

items.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
items.description: Tasmania's top health official says the Launceston General Hospital is experiencing "impacted" capacity and is urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

items.description: Survivors and first responders reported locked doors and a lack of signage marking emergency exits.

items.title: Benin temporarily eliminated electoral commission pending reform

items.title: EU bans purchase and import of Sudanese gold in bid to curb war economy
items.description: The European Union is banning the purchase, import and transfer of Sudanese gold in a bid to curb sources of financing for the country’s devastating conflict.

items.title: King’s Swan Upping on the Thames shows healthy cygnets but rising litter

This edition showcased over 6,000 troops, including 500 from the coalition of the willing. France's rearmament and Europe's strategic autonomy on full display. The event aims to show the US president and Russia's Vladimir Putin that Europe is united and stands ready to defend itself. For more, FRANCE 24 spoke to Paul Smith, head of Modern Languages, university of Nottingham.

items.title: French city of Nice marks 10th anniversary of terrorist attack
items.description: France marks the 10-year anniversary of the 2016 Nice Bastille Day truck attack. The attacker, Mohamed Lahouaiej Bouhlel, drove his truck into a crowd along the “promenade des Anglais" who had gathered to watch fireworks - killing 86 people. While Bouhlel was killed by police on the same night, eight people are accused of helping him with the attack. Their trial will begin on Monday in a special terrorism court in Paris. Catherine Viette takes a look back at the events of that night.

items.description: Former Senegalese President Macky Sall will return to Senegal to meet his successor Bassirou Diomaye Faye for the first time since leaving power, as he seeks Dakar’s support for his candidacy to become United Nations secretary-general.

items.title: Identity of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 child abuse offences revealed
items.description: Hamish Tait is accused of abusing 136 children at five childcare facilities over a 16-year period.

items.title: Spy agency warns active Russian hackers targeting Australian industry
items.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

items.title: China expels senior Communist party leader in anti-corruption crackdown
items.description: Ma Xingrui is the third member of the 24-member Politburo to come under investigation during the current party term that began in 2022, a rare occurrence in the recent history of the 105-year-old party

items.title: As the world rearms, South Korea aims to cash in
items.description: Weapons and military equipment produced in South Korea are sought-after due to their reliability, proven effectiveness, short delivery times and relatively low prices.

items.title: The breaking of nations
items.description: From ancient times, periods of relative stability in which one big power was strong enough to maintain a semblance of order in the region it dominated have alternated with others marked by strife like the one we appear to be sliding into right now. Leer más

items.title: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
items.description: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

items.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
items.description: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

items.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
items.description: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

items.title: Death toll from a Bangkok music bar fire rises to 30, dozens remain in hospital
items.description: More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.

items.title: Ukraine targets another 11 Russian ships in Sea of Azov
items.description: Ukraine said it struck tankers and cargo vessels in the Sea of Azov, in the latest strikes on Russian commercial shipping. Moscow's Agriculture Ministry said "alternative shipping routes" are being sought.

items.title: Africa’s coffee, cocoa and tea farmers face growing risks as prices swing
items.description: Millions of smallholder farmers depend on coffee, cocoa and tea for their livelihoods. The FAO warns that climate shocks, diseases and market instability are increasing pressure on producers, especially in African countries where these crops are key sources of income.

items.title: Germany: Left-wing group says it is behind railway sabotage
items.description: The "Angry Birds Commando" group said they placed incendiary devices on a railroad between the two busy cities of Cologne and Düsseldorf. The group blamed industrial technology for the destruction of the environment.

items.title: Investigators probe a fire at a music bar in Bangkok that killed at least 27 people
items.description: By Monday morning, forensic teams were investigating the scene. Officials say many victims were trapped in windowless bathrooms near a rear exit. Investigators are focusing on the ceiling above the performance stage for clues.

items.title: Germany's Ahr Valley: Rebuilding better after deadly flood
items.description: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: Towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

items.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
items.description: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

items.title: Argentina sink 10-man Swiss to set up England World Cup semi-final clash

items.description: When completed, the laboratory is expected to serve as a national hub for agricultural research and innovation, supporting advances in food safety, biotechnology, biosecurity and environmental monitoring. items.title: U.S., Iran trade claims over Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate
items.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard rejected that claim, declaring the strait to be Iranian territory and warning that it would not tolerate what it called illegal U.S. interference.

items.title: US-Iraq relations: Can Iran-allied militias be disarmed?
items.description: When Donald Trump meets Iraq's new PM today, they are likely to discuss disarming Iran's allies in Iraq. Some have already pledged to give up their guns. But is that likely? And will it influence the US-Iran war?

items.title: US: 12 states challenge Paramount's buyout of Warner
items.description: The states have filed a lawsuit arguing that a merger of Hollywood giant studios Paramount and Warner would "extinguish competition" and increase consumer prices.

items.title: World Cup culture wars and the legend of Lionel Messi

items.title: How people’s genes are revealing why some languages are so unique
items.description: A new study measured how different neighbouring languages were from one another. The authors say that linguistic hotspots may offer glimpses into how human languages became more varied before large migrations and population movements reshaped much of the world

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes for the third day
items.description: A serious escalation is occurring in the fighting between the US and Iran. Overnight and into this morning, the American military targeted sites across the Islamic Republic – while Iran fired ballistic missiles at US airbases in Jordan and Bahrain. There has been an escalation in the war of words too: President Donald Trump vowed to reinstate an American blockade of Iranian ports and charge ships for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Details and analysis by Scott Lucas, professor of US and international politics at the Clinton Institute of University College Dublin.

items.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuks has been fasting in protest of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan since over two weeks. As his health deteriorates, the pressure piles up on Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

items.title: Spain's Lamine Yamal warns France: 'we have no fear' ahead of World Cup semifinal
items.description: The 2026 Football World Cup is in its final stretch. Tomorrow, England will take on Argentina for a place in the finals. And this evening, France will play Spain in their semifinals match. French coach Didier Deschamps predicted a “spectacular” match, while Spanish star Lamine Yamal shared a warning to Les Bleus.
